ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for pandan chiffon cake or Singaporean cake-and-beverage set meals. FOODON_00002400 (cake food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cakes, pastries and similar baked products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cakes and pastries as a category. No specific code for pandan chiffon cake or set meals combining cake with hot beverage. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Cake) used as closest parent. No specific code for pandan chiffon cake or set meal.

preparation_method: hot beverage brewed or prepared separately
main_ingredients: hot beverage (coffee or tea, milk-based or black)

serving.description: 1 set (1 slice pandan chiffon cake + 1 medium hot beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of one slice of pandan chiffon cake (approximately 80–100 g) paired with one medium-sized hot beverage (approximately 220–240 ml, typically kopi, teh, or Milo), totalling approximately 320 g or ml combined.

nutrient_source.title: Pandan Chiffon Cake — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central Chiffon Cake SR Legacy; Hot Beverage (Kopi/Teh) — HPB Singapore Foodo
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for the set are estimated as a weighted composite: pandan chiffon cake slice (~90 g, per HPB Foodo 'Pandan Chiffon Cake' entry) and a medium hot kopi-o or teh-o (~230 ml, per HPB Foodo beverage entries). Per-100g values are derived from the combined set weight of approximately 320 g. The pandan chiffon cake component dominates the macronutrient profile. HPB Foodo reports pandan chiffon cake at approximately 340 kcal/100g, protein 5.5 g, fat 12.5 g, carbohydrate 52 g. The hot beverage (unsweetened kopi-o or teh-o) contributes negligible macronutrients at typical dilution. Composite per-100g values are calculated accordingly.

gi_source.title: Chiffon cake, pandan flavoured — GI analogous estimate based on 'Sponge cake' and 'Vanilla cake' entries; University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for pandan chiffon cake in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 67 based on analogous sponge cake and vanilla chiffon cake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 63–72 for similar egg-and-flour-based cakes with moderate sugar content. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 32.5) / 100 ≈ 21.8, using a 90 g cake slice contributing approximately 32.5 g available carbohydrate. The hot beverage (unsweetened kopi-o or teh-o) contributes negligible carbohydrate to GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content and added sugar
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this set to occasional consumption, as the cake contributes approximately 95 mg sodium per 100 g and a significant sugar load; choosing an unsweetened hot beverage (kopi-o kosong or teh-o kosong) reduces the overall sugar and caloric burden of the set.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this set sparingly, as the pandan chiffon cake has a medium GI of approximately 67 and delivers an estimated glycemic load of 21.8 per serving from refined flour and added sugar; pairing with an unsweetened beverage and limiting portion to half a slice can help moderate postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk and eggs, and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful that pandan chiffon cake contains approximately 4.1 g saturated fat per 100 g primarily from coconut milk and egg yolks, and the high sugar content may contribute to elevated triglycerides; occasional consumption as part of a heart-healthy diet is acceptable if overall saturated fat and sugar intake is managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan Chiffon Cake Set with Medium Hot Beverage is a popular Singaporean café set meal offered at local toast and coffee chains such as Toast Box. The set comprises one slice of pandan chiffon cake paired with a medium-sized hot beverage, typically kopi (local Robusta coffee with condensed or evaporated milk), teh (local milk tea), Milo, or their unsweetened variants (kopi-o, teh-o). Pandan chiffon cake is a light, airy, and moist sponge-style cake made by folding stiffly beaten egg whites into a batter of cake flour, egg yolks, sugar, coconut milk, vegetable oil, and freshly extracted pandan (screwpine leaf) juice. The pandan juice imparts a distinctive bright green colour and a fragrant, subtly sweet, vanilla-like aroma that is characteristic of Southeast Asian baking. The texture is exceptionally soft and fluffy due to the chiffon technique, which relies on the structural support of the tube pan and the volume of the meringue. Nutritionally, the cake is moderately energy-dense at approximately 265 kcal per 100 g of the combined set, with carbohydrates (predominantly refined flour and added sugar) as the dominant macronutrient at around 40.5 g per 100 g. The coconut milk and eggs contribute saturated fat (approximately 4.1 g per 100 g) and cholesterol. The glycemic index is estimated at 67 (medium), making the set a moderate-to-high glycemic meal. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size and choose unsweetened beverage options to reduce postprandial glucose response. Those managing cardiovascular disease or hypertension should be aware of the saturated fat and sugar content. The set is a culturally significant comfort food in Singapore, commonly consumed at breakfast or as an afternoon snack in traditional kopitiam-style café settings.
common_user_queries: How many calories in pandan chiffon cake set?
What is the GI of pandan chiffon cake?
Can diabetics eat pandan chiffon cake?
What is in a Toast Box pandan cake set?
What does pandan chiffon cake taste like?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for 'Pandan Chiffon Cake Set with Medium Hot Beverage' as a composite set meal exists in HPB Foodo or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted composite of HPB Foodo pandan chiffon cake data and HPB Foodo hot beverage data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable sponge and chiffon cake entries. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation methodology.
notes: 1. This is a set meal record combining pandan chiffon cake (~90 g slice) and a medium hot beverage (~230 ml). Per-100g values are calculated from the combined set weight of approximately 320 g. The cake component dominates the macronutrient profile. 2. The hot beverage component is assumed to be an unsweetened variant (kopi-o or teh-o) for nutrient calculation purposes; if a sweetened beverage (kopi or teh with condensed milk) is chosen, energy, sugar, and carbohydrate values will be higher. 3. Vitamin B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry for pandan chiffon cake and are not reliably estimable for the composite set. 4. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for pandan chiffon cake was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 5. The set is served at Toast Box - Funan (field survey data source). 6. Allergen profile assumes standard recipe; individual restaurant preparation may vary. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy chiffon/sponge cake entries as supplementary reference where HPB Foodo does not report these values.
